research.status_summary: Actively hiring across 148+ open roles in Israel (Herzliya, Haifa, Tel Aviv, Jerusalem) as of June 2026, with no layoffs at the Israeli R&D centers — explicitly bucking the broader Big Tech layoff wave.
research.hiring_policy: Roles are on-premises or hybrid (at least 3 days/week in Tel Aviv office); Apple Israel hires directly as a local legal entity — no EOR arrangement needed — and does not sponsor relocation visas for most Israel-based roles, which are designed for local Israeli talent.
recent_news: Jan 2026 — Apple acquired Tel Aviv-based AI startup Q.ai (~$2B, Apple's largest deal since Beats 2014); ~100 employees + founders Aviad Maizels, Yonatan Wexler, Avi Barliya absorbed into Apple Israel, deepening audio/ML and silent-speech AI capabilities.

Mar 2026 — iPhone 17e launched with C1X cellular modem and N1 wireless chip (Wi-Fi 7 + Bluetooth), both developed with significant engineering input from Apple's Israel R&D centers in Haifa and Herzliya.
Apr 2026 — Johny Srouji (who oversees Israel engineering teams) promoted to Chief Hardware Officer, signaling elevated strategic importance of Apple's Israeli semiconductor and hardware operations.
research.growth_signal: Apple Israel's R&D centers in Herzliya, Haifa, and Jerusalem remain stable and actively hiring across silicon, ML/AI, and generative AI roles while peer companies (Meta, Intuit, ZoomInfo) are cutting Israeli headcount — a clear counter-cyclical expansion signal.
research.fit_for_lior: Apple Israel's open 'Full Stack Engineer – Generative AI for Productivity' role (Haifa), the ML-Physics/physics-informed neural networks team (Herzliya), and the speech/audio AI pipeline flowing from the Q.ai acquisition are directly aligned with Lior's Creative AI and Vibe Coder threads, though the org skews toward deep-engineering profiles rather than pure product/analyst tracks.
